input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges="None"` only affects **iOS safe area insets**. You likely need to address these additional areas:

**1. Hide the app's navigation bar** (the most common culprit):
```xml
<!-- If using Shell -->
<ContentPage Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False" ...>

<!-- If using NavigationPage -->
<ContentPage NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False" ...>
```

**2. Android: opt into edge-to-edge drawing.**
In your `MainActivity.cs`:
```csharp
protected override void OnCreate(Bundle? savedInstanceState)
{
    base.OnCreate(savedInstanceState);
    AndroidX.Core.View.WindowCompat.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(Window, false);
}
```
This tells Android to draw your content behind the system status and navigation bars. (On Android 15+ / API 35 this is the default, but earlier versions require it.)

**3. Make system bars translucent** so the image shows through:
```csharp
// In OnCreate after the above call:
Window.SetStatusBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
Window.SetNavigationBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
```

**4. Your XAML page** should look something like:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;..."
             ios:Page.UseSafeArea="False"
             Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False">
    <Image Source="photo.jpg"
           Aspect="AspectFill" />
</ContentPage>
```

> **TL;DR:** `SafeAreaEdges`/`UseSafeArea` handles iOS. Android needs `SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(Window, false)` + transparent system bar colors. And make sure the app's own nav bar is hidden.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about making content go edge-to-edge in a .NET MAUI app, going behind the status bar and navigation bar. They've tried `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the ContentPage but still see padding.

This is a common .NET MAUI question. The key issue is that `SafeAreaEdges` alone isn't enough — you also need to enable edge-to-edge rendering at the platform level.

In .NET MAUI / .NET 10, there are a few things needed:

1. On **iOS**, `SafeAreaEdges="None"` should work to ignore safe area insets, but you may also need to set the page to extend under the status bar.


2. On **Android**, the approach is different — edge-to-edge rendering requires platform-specific configuration, especially for versions before Android 15 where it's now enforced by default. You'll need to handle system bars separately rather than relying on the safe area properties alone.

In .NET MAUI, the key issue is that `SafeAreaEdges="None"` only addresses iOS safe area insets, while Android requires additional setup to draw behind system bars. If you're using Shell or NavigationPage, the app's own navigation bar also contributes spacing that needs to be accounted for separately.
